name: Venice (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the field of the Republic of Venice's banner
the gold of the Lion of Saint Mark

description: A red field bearing the gold winged Lion of Saint Mark holding an open book, with six tongue-shaped tails (le code) extending from the fly.
history: The Lion of Saint Mark on red has been the banner of Venice since the medieval period, used continuously by the Republic of Venice (697 to 1797) and revived as the city flag of modern Venice. The six tails reference the six sestieri (districts) of the historic city.

features.role: Winged Lion of Saint Mark holding an open book
features.symbolism: the patron saint of Venice; the open book bears the inscription 'Pax tibi Marce, evangelista meus'
Six tongue-shaped tails (le code) extending from the fly, referencing the six sestieri of historic Venice.
